sbol:ComponentDefinition.dcterms:description: green fluorescent protein derived from jellyfish Aequeora victoria wild-type GFP (SwissProt: P42212
sbol:ComponentDefinition.dcterms:title: cds_BBa_E0040
sbol:ComponentDefinition.dc:creator: jcbraff
sbol:ComponentDefinition.ns1:mutableDescription: GFP (mut3b) [note that this part does not have a barcode]

sbol:ComponentDefinition.dc:creator: Vinay S Mahajan, Voichita D. Marinescu, Brian Chow, Alexander D Wissner-Gross and Peter Carr IAP, 2003.
sbol:ComponentDefinition.ns1:mutableNotes:
Varies from -6 to +1 region from original sequence to accomodate BioBricks suffix.

No secondary structures are formed in the given RBS region. Users should check for secondary structures induced in the RBS by upstream and downstream elements in the +50 to -50 region, as such structures will greatly affect the strength of the RBS. Contact info for this part: Brian Chow
sbol:ComponentDefinition.dcterms:title: rbs_BBa_B0034

sbol:ComponentDefinition.ns1:mutableDescription: Double terminator consisting of BBa_B0010 and BBa_B0012
sbol:ComponentDefinition.dcterms:title: terminator_BBa_B0015
sbol:ComponentDefinition.dc:creator: Reshma Shetty

sbol:ComponentDefinition.dcterms:title: cds_BBa_C0040
sbol:ComponentDefinition.dc:creator: June Rhee, Connie Tao, Ty Thomson, Louis Waldman.
sbol:ComponentDefinition.ns1:mutableDescription: Coding region for the TetR protein without the Ribosome Binding Site. Modified with an LVA tail for rapid degradation of the protein and faster fall time for the emission. TetR binds to the pTet regulator (BBa_R0040). aTc (anhydrotetracycline) binds to TetR and inhibits its operation.
sbol:ComponentDefinition.ns1:mutableProvenance: Elowitz, M. B. Transport, Assembly, and Dynamics in Systems of Interacting Proteins. Thesis, Princeton Univ., Princeton (1999)
sbol:ComponentDefinition.ns1:mutableNotes:
References (unparsed) here:

Elowitz, M. B. Transport, Assembly, and Dynamics in Systems of Interacting Proteins. Thesis, Princeton Univ., Princeton (1999).

Lutz R, Bujard H., Independent and tight regulation of transcriptional units in Escherichia coli via the LacR/O, the TetR/O and AraC/I1-I2 regulatory elements. Nucleic Acids Res. 1997 Mar 15;25(6):1203-10. PMID: 9092630

References (unparsed) here:

Elowitz, M. B. Transport, Assembly, and Dynamics in Systems of Interacting Proteins. Thesis, Princeton Univ., Princeton (1999).

Lutz R, Bujard H., Independent and tight regulation of transcriptional units in Escherichia coli via the LacR/O, the TetR/O and AraC/I1-I2 regulatory elements. Nucleic Acids Res. 1997 Mar 15;25(6):1203-10. PMID: 9092630

BBa_C0040 TetR Protein is based on the TetR sequence from Elowitz's repressilator. It has been modified to include a rapid degradation LVA tail, and includes the BioBrick standard assembly head and tail restriction sites. The RBS has been removed. The stop codon has been changed from TAA to a double stop codon TAATAA.
